costs: Cost of running parallel comprehensive and control-region infrastructures for multiple years
Research/evaluation costs for rigorous matched-comparison design
Risk of pilot fatigue or under-enrollment if no maintainers volunteer given demonstrated status-quo attachment among urban/advantaged stakeholders
content.equity_impact: Uncertain but designed to generate equity evidence: if pilot follows the Finnish/Polish pattern, it could show reduced between-school variance and inequality; however, without pairing with choice/zoning reform, gains may be undermined by parallel segregation channels as seen in Finland post-1990s.
expected_benefits: Generates Hungary-specific causal evidence disentangling tracking effects from confounding factors, addressing the contested nature of mean-performance findings
Politically lower-risk than national mandate since participation is voluntary and reversible
Builds an evidence base that could inform T2-style national reform if pilot results are favorable
content.goal: Test a voluntary regional pilot removing academic selection at grades 5/7 in favor of comprehensive lower-secondary schooling with within-school enrichment, evaluated against comparison regions before any national decision.

implementation_steps.action: Select 3-5 comparable pilot and control regions matched on demographic and SES composition, design comprehensive grade 5-9 curriculum with enrichment tracks
implementation_steps.actor: Ministry of Education and volunteering county/municipal maintainers

implementation_steps.action: Implement comprehensive lower-secondary structure in pilot regions, retaining existing structure in matched control regions
implementation_steps.timeline: Years 2-6 (full cohort cycle through lower secondary)

implementation_steps.action: Conduct matched-comparison evaluation of achievement, segregation, and equity indicators across pilot vs control regions
implementation_steps.actor: Independent research bodies (CEU, TARKI, Corvinus) and National ABC infrastructure
implementation_steps.timeline: Years 2-7, with interim reports at Year 4

mechanisms: Finland's comprehensive-schooling shift (1972-1977) was associated with rising performance and historically low between-school variance, though confounded by simultaneous teacher-training, curriculum, and welfare reforms — a pilot can help isolate Hungary-specific effects from these confounds

No significant measured efficiency losses from de-tracking are found in cross-national meta-analytic evidence, and countries increasing school heterogeneity showed higher student competency progress, supporting a testable hypothesis for the pilot

Finland's post-1990s experience shows school-choice deregulation can re-introduce segregation even without formal tracking, so the pilot must pair de-tracking with choice/zoning controls (linking to T4) to be a valid test
risks: Finnish experience shows de-tracking alone is insufficient against choice-driven segregation absent institutional guardrails, so a pilot without accompanying zoning controls may understate or overstate feasible national effects
Low institutional durability precedent (Poland's reversal after measured success) suggests even a successful pilot may not translate into lasting national policy
Pilot regions volunteering may not be representative, limiting external validity of results
content.title: Comprehensive lower-secondary pilot with opt-in enrichment (Finnish-inspired de-tracking pilot)
